input_row.title: Ensure server parameter 'audit_log_events' has 'CONNECTION' set for MySQL Database Server
input_row.description: Set audit_log_enabled to include CONNECTION on MySQL Servers.
input_row.rationale: Enabling CONNECTION helps MySQL Database to log items such as successful and failed connection attempts to the server. Log data can be used to identify, troubleshoot, and repair configuration errors and suboptimal performance. Impact: There are further costs incurred for storage of logs. For high traffic databases these logs will be significant. Determine your organization's needs before enabling.
input_row.audit: From Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Azure Database for MySQL servers. 3. Select a database. 4. Under Settings, select Server parameters. 5. Ensure audit_log_enabled parameter is set to ON. 6. Ensure audit_log_events parameter has CONNECTION checked.
input_row.remediation: From Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Azure Database for MySQL servers. 3. Select a database. 4. Under Settings, select Server parameters. 5. Update audit_log_enabled parameter to ON. 6. Update audit_log_events parameter to have at least CONNECTION checked. 7. Click Save. 8. Under Monitoring, select Diagnostic settings. 9. Select + Add diagnostic setting. 10. Provide a diagnostic setting name. 11. Under Categories, select MySQL Audit Logs. 12. Specify destination details. 13. Click Save. It may take up to 10 minutes for the logs to appear in the configured destination. Default Value: By default audit_log_events is disabled. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/mysql/single-server/how-to-configure- audit-logs-portal 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-3-enable-logging-for-security-investigation Additional Information: There is also a CLI version: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/mysql/single- server/how-to-configure-audit-logs-cli
input_row.impact: There are further costs incurred for storage of logs. For high traffic databases these logs will be significant. Determine your organization's needs before enabling.
input_row.reference: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/mysql/single-server/how-to-configure- audit-logs-portal 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-3-enable-logging-for-security-investigation Additional Information: There is also a CLI version: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/mysql/single- server/how-to-configure-audit-logs-cli

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified via Azure Resource Manager APIs/CLI/SDK/PowerShell. The audit mentions specific server parameters, including `audit_log_enabled` and `audit_log_events`, which can be queried using Azure APIs for MySQL Database Servers.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_mysql_diagnostics_audit_logs_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

- **Azure Resource Manager APIs to use:** 
  - The Azure MySQL Database API, specifically the endpoint for server parameters.
  
- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Azure Database for MySQL servers, located within specific subscriptions and resource groups.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - The `audit_log_enabled` should be set to `ON`.
  - The `audit_log_events` should include `CONNECTION`.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** 
    - `audit_log_enabled` = `ON`
    - `audit_log_events` includes `CONNECTION`
  - **Fail:** If any of the above conditions are not met.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - API Endpoint: `GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.DBforMySQL/servers/{serverName}/serverParameters?api-version=2021-05-01`
  - To validate using Azure CLI:
    ```bash
    az mysql server parameter show --name audit_log_events --resource-group {resourceGroupName} --server-name {serverName}
    az mysql server parameter show --name audit_log_enabled --resource-group {resourceGroupName} --server-name {serverName}
    ```
